items.q: What is the key difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
items.correct: A compiler translates the whole program first; an interpreter executes line by line.
items.misconception: A compiler is a person, an interpreter is software.
items.why: Students confuse the human meanings with technical ones. In CS, both are programs. Compilers produce an executable; interpreters run source directly each time.

items.q: What is the difference between an IP address and a MAC address?
items.correct: MAC is a hardware identifier (fixed); IP is a logical network address (assignable).
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: IP is for input, MAC is for output.
items.why: Students treat these as synonyms. MAC is burned into the network card (Layer 2); IP is assigned by the network (Layer 3). Both are needed to route packets to a device.

items.q: What is the time complexity of binary search?
items.misconception: O(n) - because it searches through items.
items.why: Students conflate 'fast' with O(1). Binary search halves the search space each step, giving logarithmic time - much better than linear, but not constant.
Correct answer: error. “luck” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: future error trend. “past values” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Number of terms in a PID controller?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
